decks.notes: This deck utilizes cheap artifacts and efficient creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of threats. By combining affinity for artifacts with aggressive strategies, it aims to win the game by deploying a high volume of threats and burn spells to put pressure on the opponent early and often. The deck also includes some disruption elements to disrupt opposing strategies while maintaining its own game plan. It focuses on deploying threats quickly and efficiently while leveraging artifact synergies to maximize its potential.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and spells, while also disrupting their game plan with removals and graveyard hate. The key strategy is to quickly build a board presence with efficient creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Refurbished Familiar, while using cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Cast Down to remove threats. In addition, the deck utilizes sacrifice effects with cards like Fanatical Offering and Makeshift Munitions to gain advantage and finish off opponents. The inclusion of Ichor Wellspring and Lembas provides card advantage and consistency, while also enabling synergies with artifacts like Drossforge Bridge.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay by utilizing low-cost creatures like Krark-Clan Shaman and Gixian Infiltrator to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Refurbished Familiar and Writhing Chrysalis provide additional value by creating more creatures. Removal spells like Cast Down and Cleansing Wildfire help control the board, while cards like Fanatical Offering and Eviscerator's Insight provide additional reach to finish off the opponent. The deck also includes some graveyard hate with Nihil Spellbomb and recursion with Pulse of Murasa. The mana base is built around artifact lands and mana fixing lands for consistency.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of low-cost white creatures, utilizing abilities like card draw and token generation to maintain momentum. Key cards like Militia Bugler help in searching for essential creatures, while cards like Rally the Peasants and Battle Screech provide a burst of damage to finish off the opponent. Leonardo, Big Brother offers a powerful anthem effect to boost the team, while Prismatic Strands can be used to protect against board wipes. The deck aims to quickly build a wide board presence and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on quickly boosting your creatures with powerful auras to overwhelm your opponent with a flurry of aggressive attacks. By giving your creatures additional abilities and increased power, such as trample and lifelink, you can quickly take control of the board and deal significant damage to your opponent. The deck also includes several ways to search for and play key auras, ensuring that you have the right tools to maintain pressure on your opponent throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through a combination of removal spells, life gain, and reusable creature abilities. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy by utilizing cards like Pestilence, Nihil Spellbomb, and Defile, while also gaining life and card advantage with cards like Heaped Harvest, Cauldron Familiar, and Ichor Wellspring. The deck also features creatures with powerful abilities like Troll of Khazad-dûm and Reckoner's Bargain to apply pressure and close out the game. The mana base includes lands that provide additional utility, such as Witch's Cottage and Bojuka Bog.

The deck focuses on using efficient creatures and disruption spells to pressure the opponent and control the board. It aims to quickly establish a board presence with cheap creatures like Kor Skyfisher and Glint Hawk, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Cast Down. The deck also includes some card advantage engines like Refurbished Familiar and Grim Bauble to maintain resources. Overall, the Orzhov Aggro deck combines aggression with disruption to control the game and win through combat damage.

This deck aims to generate a lot of mana quickly through cards like Seething Song and Thornscape Familiar, allowing for explosive plays with cards like Big Score and Seize the Storm to storm off and win the game in one turn. The key concept is to ramp up mana and chain spells together to create a powerful storm combo that overwhelms the opponent and secures victory.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through the use of counterspells, card draw, and flicker effects while gaining life with God-Pharaoh's Faithful. Ephemerate is a key card that allows for repeated value and triggers with creatures like Archaeomancer and Sunscape Familiar. The deck focuses on establishing a board presence and outlasting opponents through card advantage and resilient threats. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the deck while also providing some utility with Contaminated Landscape and Mortuary Mire.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost creatures and various equipment to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggression. By combining efficient creatures like Novice Inspector and Glint Hawk with equipment like Tithing Blade and Greatsword of Tyr, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Additionally, cards like Duress and Cast Down help to disrupt the opponent's strategy, allowing the aggro strategy to push through to victory. The mana base provides a good balance of white and black sources, ensuring consistency in casting the deck's spells.

The key concept of this Aggro - Affinity deck is to quickly flood the battlefield with artifact creatures and utilize cards that benefit from having multiple artifacts in play, such as Galvanic Blast and Reckoner's Bargain. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a high number of low-cost creatures while taking advantage of the synergy between artifacts and graveyard recursion cards like Refurbished Familiar. By utilizing a mix of direct damage, card draw, and artifact recursion, this deck seeks to establish board dominance and close out games quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast, aggressive strategy. By playing low-cost artifacts like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar, the deck aims to flood the board with creatures and then use cards like Galvanic Blast and Krark-Clan Shaman to control the opponent's board. Thoughtcast helps to draw more cards, while Reckoner's Bargain and Toxin Analysis provide additional value. The deck also includes some utility cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Cryogen Relic to disrupt the opponent's strategy. With a focus on speed and aggression, this deck aims to win the game quickly before the opponent has a chance to recover.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Urzatron lands (Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of mana. This allows for powerful spells and creatures to be played ahead of schedule, putting pressure on the opponent while also controlling the board with removal spells and artifacts. The deck also has options for card draw and recursion to maintain card advantage and resilience. By utilizing the Urzatron efficiently, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and control the game until victory is achieved.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through efficient mana ramp and card draw, while using the Urzatron lands to generate a large amount of mana to cast powerful spells and creatures. The deck utilizes various artifacts and creature abilities to manipulate the board state and out value opponents. The end goal is to overwhelm the opponent with large threats like Bramble Wurm or Burn spells like Kaervek's Torch, while maintaining control through counterspells and removal spells. The key is to always stay ahead on resources and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a board presence with creatures like Murmuring Mystic and Wormfang Drake. It also utilizes card draw and ramp spells to maintain card advantage and mana efficiency. The inclusion of countermagic and removal spells like Cast Down and Snuff Out help in dealing with threats effectively. The deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent consistently and win through a combination of creature damage and spell disruption. It prioritizes resource management and timing to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using defender creatures like Overgrown Battlement and Axebane Guardian, then use combo pieces like Balustrade Spy to mill your entire deck and win the game with cards like Dread Return or Lotleth Giant. The deck also includes card draw and creature recursion to help support the combo strategy. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a sudden burst of mana and combo off for a quick victory.
